notes: 4 clues per country, ordered hardest/most obscure -> most obvious. Includes duplicate-name aliases from the original DATA object (Czechia/Czech Rep., North Macedonia/Macedonia) so both keys resolve to the same facts.

clues: It's made up of four constituent nations under one crown.
It's home to the world's oldest underground railway system, opened in 1863.
It gave the world the sport of football (soccer) in its modern codified form.
Big Ben and the Houses of Parliament sit along its river Thames.
It's the most visited country in the world by international tourists.
It's home to the world's most-visited museum, the Louvre.
The metric system was created here in the 1790s.
The Eiffel Tower stands in its capital.
It's home to Oktoberfest, the world's largest beer festival.
It has no overall speed limit on many stretches of its highway system, the Autobahn.
It's the most populous country in the European Union.
Its capital was once divided by a famous wall.
It's home to more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than almost any other country.
It's the world's leading producer of olive oil.
Its capital, at over 2,100 feet, is one of the highest-elevation capitals in Europe.
Flamenco and bullfighting are two of its best-known cultural traditions.
It's the oldest nation-state in Europe with unchanged borders, dating to the 12th century.
It's the world's largest producer of cork.
Its explorers helped kick off the Age of Discovery in the 15th century.
It sits on the westernmost edge of mainland Europe.
It's home to more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country on Earth.
